case_summary: Driveway gate motor smells of burnt electronics after a load-shedding spike; motor will not engage.

ground_truth.notes: Load-shedding surge damage commonly takes out gate motors. Tests that the system routes to gate_motor_fault (the equipment that failed) rather than to load_shedding_surge (Electrical) — the user-named equipment guard should anchor on the gate.

inputs.user_text: After last night's load shedding our driveway gate motor isn't working any more. There's a strong burnt-electronics smell when you stand next to the motor housing, and the gate won't move using the remote or the wall button. Nothing visibly damaged on the gate arm itself.
